name: Fuente del Caño Circular Trail
name_original: De Bolaños de Calatrava a la Virgen del Monte — circular por el camino de los Calares
description: Jog the moderate 8.9-mile Fuente del Caño Circular Trail, gaining 270 feet, and discover the unique Fuente de los Cien Caños.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Bolaños De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by gentle undulations and open countryside, offering accessible paths for various fitness levels. The region features a mix of agricultural lands and natural areas, with routes often passing by local landmarks. Many trails are suitable for beginner jogging routes Bolaños De Calatrava, providing a straightforward experience without significant elevation changes. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, making it ideal for consistent running. Best jogging routes around Bolaños De Calatrava The most popular jogging route is Fuente del Caño Circular Trail, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Ermita del Calvario – Virgen del Monte loop from Bolaños de Calatrava, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) path. This route connects two significant local sites, providing varied scenery. Local runners also love the Virgen del Monte Garden loop from Bolaños de Calatrava, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through garden areas and local surroundings, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Bolaños De Calatrava is defined by open fields, local landmarks, and gentle inclines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes Bolaños De Calatrava to more challenging moderate paths. The routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Bolaños De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Bolaños De Calatrava, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels. The komoot community has mapped 24 routes in total.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211516448/fuente-del-cano-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40639907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Monte Garden loop from Bolaños de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The running trails in Bolaños De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by gentle undulations and open countryside. You'll often pass through agricultural lands and natural areas, with routes offering views of local landmarks and the region's characteristic flat to gently rolling terrain.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginner runners?
A: Absolutely. The region offers many accessible paths suitable for various fitness levels, including easy jogging routes. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, making it ideal for consistent running without significant elevation changes, perfect for beginners.

Q: What are the typical distances of running routes in the area?
A: Running routes in Bolaños De Calatrava vary in length. You can find shorter paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt; at approximately 5 km (3.1 miles), to longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211516448/fuente-del-cano-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; which is about 14.3 km (8.9 miles).

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass by significant local sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Calvario – Virgen del Monte loop from Bolaños de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; connects two important local landmarks. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt; or religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt; near some trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: Many outdoor trails in the region are dog-friendly, especially those in open countryside. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to check specific route regulations if available.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The running routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain and the accessibility of the paths, making them enjoyable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any natural monuments to see while running?
A: Yes, the region is known for its volcanic landscape. You might find trails that offer views of natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave&lt;/a&gt; is another interesting natural feature in the area.

Q: Where can I find running routes with parking options?
A: Many running routes in Bolaños De Calatrava start from or pass through towns and villages where public parking is generally available. For routes starting in Bolaños de Calatrava itself, you can typically find parking within the town or at designated spots near trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Bolaños De Calatrava, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are usually mild, making year-round running possible, though some paths might be muddy after rain.
tips: Today, this castle, which dates back to the 13th century, serves as a museum and has panels with QR codes so you can listen to an audio guide in Spanish and English. It also offers dramatized tours and guided tours, during which you can walk around its extensive wall.
3 kilometer section that does not lead from Bolaños de Calatraba to Virguen de los Montes, a good road.

The Sanctuary of the Virgen del Monte is a complex in which the Marian image of the Virgen del Monte is venerated, in the municipality of Bolaños de Calatrava. It consists of two hermitages, one primitive from the 13th century and another later from the end of the 19th century and beginning of the 20th century.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Caño Circular Trail is primarily a greenway, meaning you can expect mostly accessible and relatively flat paths. It's well-suited for jogging, offering a smooth experience through the countryside.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features a few interesting spots. You'll pass by the Gardens of the Castle of Bolaños de Calatrava, offering a glimpse of local history. A significant portion of the trail follows the Greenway from Bolaños to the Virgen del Monte, which is pleasant for jogging. You'll also encounter the Virgen del Monte Garden, a serene spot.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, given the trail's starting point near Bolaños de Calatrava and its greenway nature, you'll likely find convenient parking options in or around Bolaños de Calatrava, especially near the start of the greenway sections.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente del Caño Circular Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: With a 'moderate' difficulty grade and relatively low elevation changes (around 82 meters up and down over 14.27 km), this trail is quite approachable. While it requires some fitness, beginners with a reasonable level of conditioning should find it manageable for jogging.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, greenways and similar trails are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near gardens. Always carry bags to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are typically ideal for jogging in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be good, but avoid the midday heat. Winters are generally mild, but check local weather for any rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Fuente del Caño Circular Trail. It's a public route, freely accessible for recreation.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while jogging?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied and pleasant countryside scenery, typical of a greenway. This includes open fields, possibly some agricultural land, and views of the local landscape as you move between Bolaños de Calatrava and the Virgen del Monte area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with the 'Ruta de la Fuente del Caño' for about 34% of its length. A smaller section (3%) also coincides with the 'Rurta entre santuarios. Santuario de la Virden del Monte. Santuario de Ntra. Sra. de las Nieves'.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, the estimated duration for the 14.27 km route is approximately 1 hour and 35 minutes. This can vary based on your pace and any stops you make.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends near Bolaños de Calatrava, you'll find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and restrooms within the town. Along the greenway sections, facilities might be limited, so plan accordingly.
